description.task: Implement a ComplexityAnalyzer class that measures and compares the runtime growth of three algorithm patterns: O(1) constant, O(n) linear, and O(n²) quadratic. Run each pattern for increasing input sizes and print the operation counts.
instructions: quadraticOps(n) must use two nested loops each running n times
Increment and return an ops counter from the inner loop body
The result of quadraticOps(n) must equal n*n
printGrowthTable() is already implemented; only quadraticOps() needs to be written
Output must match the expected table format exactly
description.hint: Two nested for loops: for i in 0..n, for j in 0..n, ops++. Return ops. With n=10 this returns 100, n=100 returns 10000.

description.task: Build a SpaceComplexityDemo class with three methods. Each method allocates memory differently: O(1) space (only a few variables), O(n) space (array proportional to n), and O(n²) space (2D array). Compute and print the number of integer slots allocated for each.
instructions: quadraticSpace(n) must create and return a new int[n][n] matrix
Fill each cell [i][j] with the value i * n + j
printSpaceUsage() is already implemented — only quadraticSpace() needs code
The returned matrix must not be null for printSpaceUsage() to work correctly
Verify O(n²) slots = n*n for each call
description.hint: int[][] matrix = new int[n][n]; Then nested loops: matrix[i][j] = i * n + j; return matrix;
description.expectedOutput:
n = 5
O(1) auxiliary slots: 2
O(n) auxiliary slots: 5
O(n^2) auxiliary slots: 25
n = 10
O(1) auxiliary slots: 2
O(n) auxiliary slots: 10
O(n^2) auxiliary slots: 100

description.task: Write a BigOClassifier class that, given a measured operation count relative to input size n, classifies it into the nearest Big O category: O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), O(n²). Implement classify(long ops, int n) using ratio comparisons.
instructions: The classify() logic is already provided in the skeleton — read it carefully
demonstrateClassifications() must call classify() at least 5 times with the test pairs specified in the comment
Print each result in the format: 'classify(ops=X, n=Y) -> Z'
Do not modify the logic inside classify()
main() tests are fixed — your demonstrateClassifications() output is additional
description.hint: In demonstrateClassifications(), loop through a 2D array of {ops, n} pairs and call classify for each, printing the formatted result string.
description.expectedOutput:
O(1)
O(log n)
O(n)
O(n log n)
O(n^2)
